description: Research-backed reference safety scores for calibration. Sources: GPI 2024, INFORM Risk Index 2024, US State Dept advisories, Transparency International CPI 2024, World Bank governance indicators.
methodology: Scores derived by averaging normalized values from GPI (peace/conflict), INFORM (risk/vulnerability), CPI (governance/corruption), and advisory levels. Each source normalized to 1-10 scale, then averaged with equal weight to produce a consensus reference score. Conflict/crisis countries anchored to known severity levels.

countries.name: Iceland
countries.rationale: GPI #1 globally, minimal crime, excellent governance, no conflict, Level 1 advisory

countries.name: Denmark
countries.rationale: GPI top-5, CPI top-3, Level 1 advisory, excellent rule of law

countries.name: Norway
countries.rationale: GPI top-20, CPI top-5, excellent governance and health systems

countries.name: Finland
countries.rationale: GPI top-15, CPI top-5, very low crime, excellent social services

countries.name: Switzerland
countries.rationale: GPI top-15, CPI top-10, neutral country, excellent governance

countries.name: Singapore
countries.rationale: GPI top-10, extremely low crime, excellent governance, Level 1 advisory

countries.name: New Zealand
countries.rationale: GPI top-5, CPI top-5, low crime, stable democracy

countries.name: Austria
countries.rationale: GPI top-10, stable, low crime, good healthcare

countries.rationale: GPI top-30, CPI top-10, good governance though rising crime concerns
